name: Qualitative answer with no numeric claims must pass even if paraphrased
input: Why did the Roman Empire fall?
rag_chunks: Historians attribute the fall of the Western Roman Empire to a combination of factors: economic strain, military overstretch, political instability, and pressure from migrating peoples.
output: The fall is generally explained by overlapping pressures — economic decline, an overextended military, instability at the top, and outside migrations weighing on the borders.
